posts.t: The boring case for SIPs over lumpsum, in three charts.
posts.excerpt: Lumpsum may win on a spreadsheet over 20 years — but most people don't behave like spreadsheets. Here's the case for the patience tax that SIPs pay.

posts.t: Why your term cover is probably half of what it should be.
posts.excerpt: Most Indians are underinsured by a factor of two. Here's how to calculate the number your family actually needs.

posts.t: FY26 tax changes — the three that actually matter.
posts.excerpt: Out of eleven budget proposals, three will materially change how you invest and file. Here's what they are.

posts.t: The 4% rule, the 25× corpus, and why both are wrong for India.
posts.excerpt: American retirement math doesn't translate to Indian inflation, Indian healthcare costs or Indian family structures.

posts.t: On the surprising boredom of getting rich slowly.
posts.excerpt: The most effective wealth-building strategy requires you to do almost nothing. That's why so few people follow it.

posts.t: Why your portfolio's worst enemy is the news app on your phone.
posts.excerpt: Every push notification that makes you feel anxious about your investments is costing you money. Here's the evidence.

posts.t: A will, a nominee, a trust — which one does what?
posts.excerpt: These three instruments are often confused. Confusing them can leave your family with years of legal work.
